steps.title: Separate fixed and recurring costs
steps.content: The \$35 activation fee is paid only once, so it is a constant. The \$23 per month is multiplied by the number of months $t$, contributing $23t$.

steps.title: Combine into a total-cost equation
steps.content: Total cost is fixed plus recurring: $c = 23t + 35$.

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A: $c = \frac{t}{23} + 35$ would mean the cost per month decreases as $t$ grows, not a flat \$23/month. Eliminated.
  • Choice B: $c = \frac{t}{35} + 23$ swaps both roles — \$23 becomes the constant and \$35 becomes a divisor. Eliminated.
  • Choice C: $c = 23t + 35$ correctly adds \$23 per month to the one-time \$35. Matches.
  • Choice D: $c = 35t + 23$ swaps the monthly rate and the activation fee. Eliminated.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
